workflow.name: Generate and schedule LinkedIn, X, and Instagram posts with OpenAI agents
workflow.description:
This workflow orchestrates three specialized AI agents in sequence to research trends, generate platform-specific content (captions + images), and schedule posts across LinkedIn, X (Twitter), and Instagram.

### Who's it for
• Social media managers handling multiple brands
• Marketing teams needing consistent content pipelines
• Creators automating their content calendar
• Agencies managing high-volume posting schedules

### How it works
1. A topic/industry keyword is submitted via webhook or schedule
2. **Agent 1 (Research):** Searches current trends, extracts insights & hashtags
3. **Agent 2 (Content Gen):** Produces platform-specific captions + image prompts
4. **Agent 3 (Scheduling):** Posts to LinkedIn, X, and Instagram via their APIs
5. All activity is logged to a Google Sheet tracker

### How to set up
1. Import this workflow into n8n
2. Set credentials: OpenAI, LinkedIn OAuth2, Twitter OAuth2, Instagram Graph API, Google Sheets OAuth2, SendGrid (optional alerts)
3. Replace YOUR_SHEET_ID placeholder in the Google Sheet node
4. Replace YOUR_INSTAGRAM_ACCOUNT_ID, YOUR_LINKEDIN_PERSON_URN placeholders
5. Activate the workflow

### Requirements
• OpenAI API key (GPT-4.1 mini or better)
• LinkedIn OAuth2 credentials
• Twitter/X OAuth2 credentials
• Instagram Graph API credentials (Business account)
• Google Sheets OAuth2
• Optional: Stable Diffusion / DALL-E for image generation

### How to customize
• Swap OpenAI model in all AI nodes
• Adjust character limits per platform in Agent 2 prompt
• Modify posting schedule in the Schedule Trigger node
• Add Slack/email notifications on post confirmation
• Extend the Google Sheet columns for additional metadata

parameters.content:
## Multi-Agent Social Media Content Factory

This workflow orchestrates three specialized AI agents in sequence to research trends, generate platform-specific content (captions + images), and schedule posts across LinkedIn, X (Twitter), and Instagram.

### Who's it for
• Social media managers handling multiple brands
• Marketing teams needing consistent content pipelines
• Creators automating their content calendar
• Agencies managing high-volume posting schedules

### How it works
1. A topic/industry keyword is submitted via webhook or schedule
2. **Agent 1 (Research):** Searches current trends, extracts insights & hashtags
3. **Agent 2 (Content Gen):** Produces platform-specific captions + image prompts
4. **Agent 3 (Scheduling):** Posts to LinkedIn, X, and Instagram via their APIs
5. All activity is logged to a Google Sheet tracker

### How to set up
1. Import this workflow into n8n
2. Set credentials: OpenAI, LinkedIn OAuth2, Twitter OAuth2, Instagram Graph API, Google Sheets OAuth2, SendGrid (optional alerts)
3. Replace YOUR_SHEET_ID placeholder in the Google Sheet node
4. Replace YOUR_INSTAGRAM_ACCOUNT_ID, YOUR_LINKEDIN_PERSON_URN placeholders
5. Activate the workflow

### Requirements
• OpenAI API key (GPT-4.1 mini or better)
• LinkedIn OAuth2 credentials
• Twitter/X OAuth2 credentials
• Instagram Graph API credentials (Business account)
• Google Sheets OAuth2
• Optional: Stable Diffusion / DALL-E for image generation

### How to customize
• Swap OpenAI model in all AI nodes
• Adjust character limits per platform in Agent 2 prompt
• Modify posting schedule in the Schedule Trigger node
• Add Slack/email notifications on post confirmation
• Extend the Google Sheet columns for additional metadata

## 1. Trigger & Input

Accepts a topic/industry keyword via:
- POST webhook (manual/external trigger)
- Scheduled cron (recurring content calendar)

Normalizes input into a standard `{ topic, industry, targetAudience }` shape before passing downstream.

## 2. Agent 1 — Trend Research

Uses web search + AI to:
- Fetch current trending topics for the given industry
- Extract key insights, stats, and talking points
- Identify platform-relevant hashtags (LinkedIn, X, Instagram)
- Output a structured JSON trend report

Fails gracefully: if search fails, uses AI general knowledge as fallback.

## 3. Agent 2 — Content Generation

Receives trend report and produces:
- LinkedIn caption (≤3000 chars, professional tone, 3-5 hashtags)
- X/Twitter caption (≤280 chars, punchy, 2-3 hashtags)
- Instagram caption (≤2200 chars, engaging, 10-15 hashtags)
- DALL-E image prompt for each platform
- Structured JSON content bundle

Skips scheduling if image generation fails (conditional gate).

## 4. Agent 3 — Scheduling & Publishing

Receives content bundle and:
- Publishes to LinkedIn via Pages API
- Posts to X via v2 Tweets API
- Schedules Instagram via Graph API (create media → publish)
- Logs post IDs, timestamps, and platform status to Google Sheet
- Sends error alerts via SendGrid if any platform fails

Each platform posts independently — one failure does NOT block others.

options.systemMessage: You are a precise JSON-outputting research agent. Output ONLY valid JSON, no markdown, no preamble, no explanation. Use your web search capability to find current trends.
You are a precise JSON-outputting content generation agent. Output ONLY valid JSON. Strictly enforce character limits — especially Twitter's 280-character hard limit. Count characters carefully.

user.name: Oneclick AI Squad
user.bio: The AI Squad Initiative is a pioneering effort to build, automate and scale AI-powered workflows using n8n.io. Our mission is to help individuals and businesses integrate AI agents seamlessly into their daily operations  from automating tasks and enhancing productivity to creating innovative, intelligent solutions. We design modular, reusable AI workflow templates that empower creators, developers and teams to supercharge their automation with minimal effort and maximum impact.

data.details: The Code node allows you to execute JavaScript in your workflow.

The Filter node can be used to filter items based on a condition. If the condition is met, the item will be passed on to the next node. If the condition is not met, the item will be omitted. Conditions can be combined together by AND(meet all conditions), or OR(meet at least one condition).
